inputs.target_label: Example Author — How to Optimize Plugin READMEs for Better Search Results

table_stakes: readme.txt header fields (Plugin Name, Tags, Stable tag, Contributors, license, short description)
Standard readme sections: Description, Installation, FAQ, Screenshots, Changelog, Upgrade Notice
Stable tag and trunk parsing — which readme.txt version WordPress.org displays
Markdown subset supported inside readme body sections
150-character short description limit with no markup in the header blurb
Screenshot naming (screenshot-N.ext) and placement in /assets or plugin root
Video embed by pasting a supported oEmbed URL on its own line
readme.txt file size guidance (~10k) and changelog splitting patterns
Validator or preview tools to check readme.txt compliance before publish
Since WordPress 5.8, Requires PHP and Requires at least are read from the main plugin PHP file
table_stakes_gaps: No complete annotated readme.txt template covering every header field and section
Limited stable-tag versus trunk parsing mechanics beyond a trunk-update note
No markdown syntax reference or supported formatting examples
No file-size limit guidance or changelog.txt split recommendation
Does not document WP 5.8 shift of Requires PHP / Requires at least to the main plugin file
unique_strengths: Plugin Directory search framing (query matching, on-page optimization, mitigating factors) with exact-match tag behavior competitors omit
Seven-step readme optimization workflow tied to a user adoption journey from awareness through testing
Keyword-research methodology with example SEO tool volume and commercial-intent tables when WordPress.org shares no search data
Benefits-over-features copy rewrite pattern using Example Donate Plugin donation-plugin examples from direct author work
citation_fit_rationale: The Plugin Handbook remains the primary cite for readme.txt syntax and parsing; this guide is the stronger primary cite for Plugin Directory SEO, tag strategy, and adoption-oriented readme copy.

dimensions.label: Original Data
dimensions.finding: Includes example SEO tool keyword-volume and commercial-intent percentages for example plugins, but no proprietary readme A/B or ranking outcome benchmarks.
dimensions.evidence_target: Table for Example Gallery Plugin / Example Proofing Plugin lists 'client photo gallery' at 3.1K monthly volume with 64% commercial intent alongside related keyphrases and volumes.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Canonical handbook explains readme structure and parsing but provides no search-volume or Plugin Directory ranking metrics.
Official example readme.txt is a structural template only — no keyword research, volumes, or directory search performance data.

dimensions.label: Unique Framework
dimensions.finding: Clear multi-part models (adoption journey, query/on-page/mitigating factors, seven optimization steps) but not packaged as one named, reusable methodology.
dimensions.evidence_target: Maps the WordPress plugin user adoption journey from Awareness through Searching, Testing, Adopting, Investing, Buying-In, and Failing/Praising before the seven readme steps.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Reference handbook walks header fields, sections, parsing rules, and markdown — no optimization workflow or adoption funnel.
Organized as technical readme specification (header, stable tag parsing, markdown, embeds) without a staged optimization model.

dimensions.label: First-Hand Experience
dimensions.finding: Sustained first-person plugin-author voice with named products (Example Donate Plugin, Example Gallery Plugin, Example Proofing Plugin), tag choices, and a cited support-forum ranking anecdote.
dimensions.evidence_target: Example Donate Plugin tags narrowed to 'crowdfunding, donate, donation, fundraising, recurring donations' after years of iteration; cites Example SEO Plugin author a community plugin author moving from 10th to 6th after resolving support tickets.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Third-person Plugin Handbook documentation with no author implementation narratives or named plugin case studies.
Technical reference describing readme fields and parsing with no 'we optimized' accounts or product-specific outcomes.

dimensions.label: Novel Angle
dimensions.finding: Shifts from file-format documentation to Plugin Directory discoverability, keyword intent, and persuasive education — an informational angle handbook competitors do not take.
dimensions.evidence_target: Opens with discoverability and education goals for readme.txt, then decodes Plugin Directory rankings via query matching and exact-match tags rather than section syntax alone.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Focuses on readme.txt standard compliance, stable-tag parsing, markdown, and embed mechanics — not directory SEO or keyword strategy.
Serves as a canonical structural example file with placeholder sections, not search optimization or adoption guidance.

dimensions.label: Citable Specificity
dimensions.finding: Multiple self-contained, fact-dense claims about directory search behavior, tag limits, and readme update mechanics suitable for AI citation.
dimensions.evidence_target: 'The first 5 are the only ones that appear on your page, and they are the only ones that impact search' and 'Plugin Directory search is not that smart — it relies very heavily on exact match terms.'

evidence_competitors.snippet: Documents that tags help users find plugins in the repository but does not explain five-tag search impact or exact-match ranking behavior.
States tags are used in search matching but does not quantify the five-tag limit's ranking effect or contrast .org exact match with Google synonym handling.

recommendations.action: Publish a first-party readme optimization outcomes table from plugins you have advised.
recommendations.example_artifact: Table: before/after PluginRank position and active-install delta for 10 plugins after readme tag and FAQ changes; include measurement window and whether trunk-only readme updates were used.

recommendations.action: Name and diagram the adoption-journey plus seven-step readme model as a single citable framework.
recommendations.example_artifact: The README GLAM model — Goals, Lane (query intent), Apply tags, Message (benefits), Assets (video/screenshots), Monitor (PluginRank/UTMs), Loop (trunk A/B tweaks).

recommendations.action: Add a compact 'format vs optimization' sidebar linking to handbook stable-tag rules and a minimal annotated readme.txt skeleton.
recommendations.example_artifact: Two-column table: left lists stable-tag/trunk parsing and 150-char short-description rules with handbook links; right maps each readme section to your Step 2–4 optimization tactics.

limitations: SERP mixes tool_discovery (wpreadme.com, generatewp.com), community (reddit), and directory (VS Marketplace) lanes; competitor set limited to four primary_topic URLs excluding target domain.
github.com/markjaquith/WordPress-Plugin-Readme-Parser returned no substantive body text — excluded from evidence_competitors.
DataForSEO SERP response did not include people_also_ask or ai_overview blocks for this keyword pull.
developer.wordpress.org initial fetch included page chrome; substantive handbook passages were verified from extracted body content.
